V-M-012.checks: Deploy and nginx changes do not introduce weak defaults or unsafe routing assumptions. Deploy creates /var/lib/krotpn/ directory for RU IP snapshots. Snapshot directory permissions are correct (root:root, 755). Phase-36 planned deploy changes must enable Resend email provider without logging RESEND_API_KEY. Phase-38 planned deploy changes must wire DE-backed MTProto edge roles without exposing policy tokens or wildcard private keys. Phase-41 local execution must restore KPprotoN fake-TLS runtime as the active data-plane while keeping official MTProxy disabled from production routing.
V-M-012.commands: bash -n deploy/deploy-on-server.sh && bash -n deploy/deploy-all.sh && bash -n deploy/quick-start.sh && bash -n deploy/create-client.sh && bash -n deploy/remove-client.sh && bash -n deploy/rollback.sh && bash -n deploy/backup-db.sh && bash -n install.sh

phase-36-verification.check-1: Clean install flow materializes EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend, RESEND_API_KEY, RESEND_API_URL, EMAIL_FROM, and EMAIL_VERIFICATION_URL_BASE into deploy configuration.
phase-36-verification.check-2: Deploy output redacts the Resend API key and never prints raw .env content.
phase-36-verification.check-3: Missing Resend API key fails before deploy claims verified-registration readiness.

phase-38-verification.check-1: Deploy surface defines RU SNI router, private RU nginx fallback on 9443, public admin on 8443, DE MTProto runtime, and private policy API roles without public policy exposure.
phase-38-verification.check-2: Phase-38 env wiring preserves existing MTProto base secret and salt on redeploy.
phase-38-verification.check-3: Deploy/install output must not print runtime tokens, wildcard private keys, raw .env values, or full proxy links.

phase-41-verification.check-1: Deploy surface selects KPprotoN fake-TLS runtime as the active DE MTProto data-plane and does not start official MTProxy as production owner.
phase-41-verification.check-2: RU public 443 SNI router sends krotpn.xyz/www.krotpn.xyz to nginx fallback and issued u-*.krotpn.xyz to DE KPprotoN runtime.
phase-41-verification.check-3: Deploy/install output must not print runtime tokens, wildcard private keys, base secret, salt, derived secrets, raw .env values, or full proxy links.

scenarios.scenario: Double deploy preserves existing WG keys and client connections
Clean deploy writes EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend and redacted Resend configuration when operator provides an API key
Deploy can materialize DE-backed MTProto edge roles while keeping HTTPS rollback available
Deploy can materialize DE KPprotoN fake-TLS edge roles while official MTProxy remains inactive in production

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-17: bash -n install.sh deploy/deploy-on-server.sh passed after Phase-36 Resend config wiring.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-17: node scripts/phase36-resend-deploy-smoke.mjs passed with installer/deploy Resend redaction checks.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 verification design adds deploy-surface gates for RU SNI router, DE runtime, private policy API, and redaction-safe TLS/token handling.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 local deploy-surface verification passed for bash syntax, HAProxy SNI router static smoke, DE runtime compose template, private policy URL wiring, and secret preservation guards.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-18: Follow-up deploy-surface checks cover HAProxy low-port bind permission and admin 8443 versus private fallback 9443 separation.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-18: Live deploy-surface proof passed after pulling GitHub main on RU, regenerating runtime nginx/HAProxy configs, and rebuilding DE runtime from the updated mtproto runtime source.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design adds deploy gates for restoring KPprotoN fake-TLS runtime and disabling official MTProxy from the active production path.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 local deploy verification passed for shell syntax, docker compose config, Phase-38/39/41 smoke scripts, and active KPprotoN DE runtime selection.
